description: Behavior cases for non-happy-path UI state design: the three distinct empties, honest loading, error placement by blast radius, partial failure, refetch/offline, and the forbidden-not-empty distinction.

cases.prompt: Our main list view just renders a blank area when there's nothing, and a spinner that spins forever if the fetch fails. Design all the states properly.

assertions: Enumerates the view's states (uninitialized, loading-first, empty variants, loaded-partial, refetching, error, offline, forbidden) and marks each IN or OUT with a reason.
Splits the empties into first-run (onboarding CTA), filtered-to-nothing (clear-filters affordance), and error-emptied (retry) with distinct copy and actions.
Chooses skeleton vs spinner vs inline for loading with a delay threshold to avoid flashing a loader on fast responses.
Designs the fetch-failure as a full-view error with retry and a correlation id, not an eternal spinner.
Keys the error copy to error-taxonomy-designer codes and hands a11y verification to accessibility-test-harness.

cases.prompt: We want toggling a setting to feel instant (optimistic), but sometimes the save fails. Design the UX for that.

assertions: Designs the optimistic update AND its rollback: what the UI shows if the write fails and how the user is told the change reverted.
Chooses an appropriate failure surface (inline/toast) with retry when the underlying error is retryable.
Avoids leaving the user believing a failed write succeeded — the revert is visible, not silent.
Does not treat optimistic UI as safe without a failure path.

cases.prompt: Our dashboard makes several independent calls. Right now if one widget's call fails, the whole page shows an error. Fix the state design.

assertions: Designs partial failure as a first-class state: working widgets render, the failed widget shows its own scoped error, and one failed call does not blank the page.
Gives the failed region its own retry rather than forcing a full-page reload.
Distinguishes a whole-view failure (all calls fail / auth lost) from a partial one.
Keeps last-good data visible on background-refetch errors rather than flashing to skeletons.

cases.prompt: For a resource the current user can't access, let's just show the same empty 'Nothing here' state we use for lists with no items.

assertions: Refuses to collapse permission-denied into the empty state: 403 'you don't have access', 404 'doesn't exist', and 'nothing here yet' are three different messages.
Flags that a wrong forbidden/empty/not-found choice can leak whether a resource exists to someone not allowed to see it, and coordinates wording with the authorization owner.
Renders forbidden with its own copy and (where appropriate) a path to request access, not a cheerful empty illustration.
Keeps the distinction consistent across views via shared components.

cases.prompt: Define the set of error codes our API returns and which ones are retryable, plus a rule that we never leak stack traces in messages.

assertions: Does not invoke edge-state-ux-designer: defining error categories, machine codes, retryable flags, and a disclosure rule is error-taxonomy-designer.
This skill would render those errors as states, but the request is about the error model, not its presentation.
